name: burp-suite-testing
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to \"intercept HTTP traffic\", \"modify web requests\", \"use Burp Suite for testing\", \"perform web vulnerability scanning\", \"test with Burp ...
capabilities: Intercept and modify HTTP requests and responses using Burp Proxy
Perform manual vulnerability testing with Repeater and Intruder tools
Configure target scope to focus testing on authorized applications
Execute automated vulnerability scans using Burp Scanner (Professional)
Analyze scan results and generate remediation reports
Test for common web vulnerabilities including SQL injection, XSS, and business logic flaws
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a legitimate Burp Suite documentation skill for authorized web application security testing. Static findings (external_commands, filesystem, network) are FALSE POSITIVES - they represent example payloads shown in a reference section for educational purposes, not actual executable code. The skill contains proper operational boundaries and best practices. Risk level set to low due to educational nature of payload examples.
valueStatement: Burp Suite is a powerful web application security testing tool, but its comprehensive features can be overwhelming. This skill provides a structured workflow for intercepting HTTP traffic, identifying vulnerabilities, and conducting professional security assessments.
userTitle: Test Web Applications with Burp Suite
limitations: Automated scanner requires Burp Suite Professional (not available in Community Edition)
Cannot test applications that use certificate pinning without additional configuration
Community Edition has limited Intruder attack capabilities
Does not test client-side vulnerabilities in compiled binaries or mobile apps

useCases.title: Security Assessment for New Web Application
useCases.description: Conduct comprehensive security testing on a newly developed web application before production deployment to identify and remediate vulnerabilities.

useCases.title: API Security Validation
useCases.description: Test REST APIs for injection vulnerabilities, authentication bypasses, and improper access controls using request modification techniques.

useCases.targetUser: Security students and aspiring penetration testers
useCases.title: Vulnerability Research and Learning
useCases.description: Learn web application security testing techniques by practicing with example vulnerabilities in controlled lab environments.

promptTemplates.title: Basic HTTP Interception
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I intercept HTTP requests using Burp Suite? Walk me through configuring the proxy and capturing traffic from a web browser.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner user wanting to understand Burp Suite proxy setup

promptTemplates.title: Testing for SQL Injection
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to use Burp Repeater to test a login form for SQL injection vulnerabilities. Include example payloads and how to interpret the responses.
promptTemplates.scenario: Intermediate user testing authentication for SQL injection

promptTemplates.title: Business Logic Testing
promptTemplates.prompt: Demonstrate how to test for business logic vulnerabilities in an e-commerce application using Burp Suite. How do I identify and exploit price manipulation issues?
promptTemplates.scenario: Intermediate user focusing on business logic flaws

promptTemplates.title: Automated Vulnerability Scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Guide me through running an automated vulnerability scan with Burp Suite Professional. How do I configure scan settings, monitor progress, and review findings?
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced user wanting to run comprehensive automated scans

bestPractices: Always define and configure target scope before starting extensive testing to avoid accidentally testing unauthorized applications
Use Burp's embedded browser for reliable proxy integration without manual browser configuration
Save your Burp project regularly to preserve work and enable collaboration with team members
antiPatterns: Do not test applications without explicit authorization - unauthorized security testing is illegal
Avoid aggressive scanning that could cause denial of service - rate-limit automated scans
Never rely solely on automated scanner results - always manually verify findings to reduce false positives

faq.question: Is Burp Suite free?
faq.answer: Burp Suite offers both Community and Professional editions. Community Edition includes Proxy, Repeater, and basic Intruder functionality. Professional Edition adds automated Scanner, advanced Intruder features, and extension support.

faq.question: Can I test HTTPS websites with Burp Suite?
faq.answer: Yes, but you need to install Burp's CA certificate in your browser to intercept HTTPS traffic. Navigate to http://burp in your configured browser to download the certificate, then add it to your browser's trusted roots.

faq.question: What is the difference between Repeater and Intruder?
faq.answer: Repeater is for manual, ad-hoc testing of individual requests where you modify and resend one request at a time. Intruder automates repeated testing with multiple payloads, useful for fuzzing, brute force attacks, and parameter enumeration.

faq.question: How do I avoid testing out-of-scope websites?
faq.answer: Configure target scope by right-clicking your target host in Target > Site map and selecting 'Add to scope'. Then enable the display filter to 'Show only in-scope items' to exclude unwanted traffic from your view.

faq.question: Why is my browser not connecting through Burp proxy?
faq.answer: Verify the proxy listener is active in Proxy > Options, confirm browser proxy settings point to 127.0.0.1:8080, check for firewall blocking local connections, or use Burp's embedded browser for reliable setup.

faq.question: Is it legal to use Burp Suite for security testing?
faq.answer: Burp Suite is a legal tool, but you must only test applications you own or have explicit written authorization to test. Unauthorized penetration testing is illegal in most jurisdictions. Always document your authorization scope.
